excerpt: The Prophet himself drew the comparison explicitly, declaring that ʿAlī's example is that of Jesus son of Mary.
source_location_hint: discussion of ʿAlī and Jesus parallels

excerpt: the Messenger of Allāh addressed the material world in similar terms, declaring triple repudiation of worldly attachments
source_location_hint: sermons on asceticism and ʿAlī's spiritual station

excerpt: Were I not apprehensive that people would say of you what the Christians said of Jesus, I would speak such words concerning your excellence.
source_authority: canonical source of ʿAlī's speeches and Prophet's sayings
source_location_hint: sermon on Prophet's restraint regarding ʿAlī's merits

excerpt: obedience to unlawful commands of scholars without Quranic warrant constitutes worship; the Christian dialogue illustrating implicit lordship through acceptance
source_location_hint: exegesis of Qur'an 9:31 on scholars as lords

excerpt: Christians erroneously elevated their Messiah to divinity; similarly, some have misunderstood the station of the Second Messiah
source_location_hint: comparative discussion of Christological error and ʿAlawī exaggeration

excerpt: Some have called him God, earning themselves the label 'Nāṣirī'—a designation of those who deify ʿAlī
